tldr: The US has launched a ninth night of strikes targeting Iran following the death of another American service member, amidst ongoing Middle East tensions.
Andy Burnham has become the new UK Prime Minister, pledging to end rough sleeping and reversing plans for digital ID.

Chinese AI companies are intensifying competition with Silicon Valley by introducing powerful and cost-effective models, while the Trump White House is reportedly considering a ban on Chinese AI models.

market.why: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite closed lower as investors remained on edge due to choppy oil prices and ongoing Middle East tensions, with semiconductors also reportedly entering a bear market.

yield_10y.why: The provided articles do not offer an explanation for the 10-year Treasury yield's slight decrease.

vix.why: The VIX increased as investors were kept on edge by fluctuating oil prices and monitoring Middle East tensions.

tech.summary: Chinese AI companies are increasing pressure on Silicon Valley by releasing models that are claimed to rival leading US versions at a lower cost, intensifying the global competition in artificial intelligence.
AMD has introduced Helios, its inaugural rack AI system, aiming to directly compete with Nvidia, and has secured Microsoft as a new customer alongside existing clients such as Meta and OpenAI.
New research indicates that AI tools used in hiring processes are more susceptible to developing biases than human recruiters, raising concerns about fairness in employment screening.

world.summary: The US has launched a ninth consecutive night of strikes targeting Iran, following the identification of additional American service members killed in an attack in Jordan, amid escalating Middle East tensions.
Andy Burnham has been appointed as the new Prime Minister of the UK, pledging to implement significant political and economic changes and expressing a commitment to ending rough sleeping.
Russian President Vladimir Putin held a meeting with North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui, prompting speculation about a possible future visit by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to Russia.
